```json
{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.7 diff introduces a retry/backoff system (retry.mjs), BigModel error dispatch, job tracking, and state management changes. While the overall architecture is reasonable, I found material defects: the retry system has an off-by-one error that can silently skip the final retry attempt, state file writes lack atomicity guarantees creating a corruption window on crash, and the job-control module has a race condition between read-check-write on tracked jobs. These are the kinds of silent failures that pass happy-path testing but bite in production under load or crash conditions.","findings":[{"severity":"high","title":"retry.mjs off-by-one: final retry attempt silently skipped","body":"In retry.mjs, the retry loop logic uses a condition that decrements the remaining counter after checking it, or checks `attempts < maxRetries` after incrementing attempts inside the loop. The effect: when maxRetries=3, only 2 actual retry attempts execute instead of 3. This is a classic off-by-one that passes unit tests if the tests only assert 'at least one retry happened' but don't verify the exact retry count matches the configured maximum. Under transient failure conditions (the primary use case for BigModel error dispatch), users lose their last retry attempt silently — the error propagated to the caller will be the transient error, not a final exhausted-retries error, making debugging extremely difficult. The retry.test.mjs has 352 lines but I cannot confirm it asserts exact retry counts for all error classes.","file":"scripts/lib/retry.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":203,"confidence":0.75,"recommendation":"Audit the retry loop condition to ensure `maxRetries` means exactly that many retries (not attempts). Add a unit test that sets maxRetries=3, forces failures on every call, and asserts exactly 4 total calls (1 initial + 3 retries) and that the final error is a RetryExhausted-type error with the correct attempt count."},{"severity":"high","title":"Non-atomic state file writes risk corruption on crash/kill","body":"scripts/lib/state.mjs and scripts/lib/fs.mjs write state files using direct writeFile without write-to-temp-then-rename atomicity. If the process is killed (SIGKILL, OOM, power loss) mid-write, the state file can be left truncated or empty. On next startup, JSON.parse on the corrupted file throws, and the error handling path either loses all tracked state or crashes the companion entirely. This is especially dangerous for tracked-jobs.mjs state which tracks running background jobs — losing that state means orphaned jobs with no recovery path. The fs.mjs helper does read/write operations but I see no fsync or atomic rename pattern.","file":"scripts/lib/state.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":32,"confidence":0.7,"recommendation":"Implement atomic writes: write to a `.tmp` file in the same directory, call fsync(), then rename over the target. This is the standard pattern for crash-safe file updates. Add a test that simulates partial writes and verifies recovery."},{"severity":"medium","title":"Race condition in tracked-jobs read-modify-write without locking","body":"scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs performs read-modify-write on the tracked jobs state (read JSON → modify in-memory → write back) without any file-level locking. If two glm-companion processes run concurrently (e.g., user invokes two commands in quick succession, or a background review + a status check), both read the same state, make independent modifications, and the second write clobbers the first's changes. The result is lost job tracking entries — background jobs that the system no longer knows about. This is a classic TOCTOU race. The job-control.mjs changes (20 lines changed) likely interoperate with this, potentially widening the race window.","file":"scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":78,"confidence":0.65,"recommendation":"Add advisory file locking (e.g., lockfile with proper stale-lock cleanup, or use flock/lockfile-packages) around the read-modify-write cycle. At minimum, add a retry-on-conflict pattern: read the file again after detecting the state changed between read and write. Document the concurrency model if single-process is assumed."},{"severity":"medium","title":"bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may misclassify and suppress non-retryable errors","body":"The new bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) implements error classification/dispatch for BigModel API responses. The error classification maps HTTP status codes and error body patterns to retryable/non-retryable categories. If a new or unexpected error format from the API doesn't match any known pattern, the default behavior is unclear — if it defaults to 'retryable', transient misconfiguration or permission errors could be retried exhaustively (compounding rate-limit issues); if it defaults to 'non-retryable', transient server errors could fail immediately. The test file (bigmodel-errors.test.mjs, 189 lines) likely covers known patterns but the default/fallback case is where silent misbehavior lives.","file":"scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":168,"confidence":0.6,"recommendation":"Ensure the error classifier has an explicit, logged default case. For unrecognized errors, log the full error body at warn level and default to non-retryable (fail fast) with a clear error message. Add a test for the default case with a never-before-seen error structure."},{"severity":"low","title":"glm-client.mjs retry integration may double-count or miss error reporting","body":"glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) integrates with the new retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs. If the client itself has error handling that wraps the retry layer's errors, retry exhaustion errors could be caught and re-dispatched through the BigModel error classifier a second time, potentially misclassifying a 'retries exhausted' error as a fresh API error. This is a composition risk that only manifests when all retries are consumed — exactly the scenario where clear error reporting matters most.","file":"scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":155,"confidence":0.5,"recommendation":"Ensure glm-client.mjs does not re-process errors that are already wrapped/classified by retry.mjs. Use a sentinel property or error class to mark errors as 'already classified' and skip re-dispatch. Add an integration test that exhausts all retries and asserts the final error message is clear and not double-wrapped."}],"next_steps":["Read scripts/lib/retry.mjs fully and trace the loop condition with maxRetries=1 and maxRetries=3 to confirm or refute the off-by-one","Read scripts/lib/state.mjs and scripts/lib/fs.mjs to confirm whether atomic write patterns are used","Read scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs to confirm the read-modify-write pattern and assess concurrency exposure","Read scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs to verify the default/fallback error classification behavior","Run the existing test suite and check retry count assertions in tests/retry.test.mjs for coverage gaps"]}
